textByHash.f319ac0ab0dd92d7713ebab9a0e7a18e8cf28e77: You don't have a sapling planted in that patch. Plant one and I might agree to look after it for you.
textByHash.8fb7dd5151767fa469a8c9065710bd252dac8218: I don't know what you're talking about - I'm already looking after that patch for you.
textByHash.afa4c7dd799e237ef8992f1ef147eb9959379c1e: That patch is already fully grown! I don't know what you want me to do with it!
textByHash.aeb9f67ee8999d8f166f56091580bd6dd9bc95eb: You can buy all the farming tools from farming shops, which can be found close to the allotments.
textByHash.2b5080fa3126d7f92fc6d4d7342716184af27023: Supercompost is far better than normal compost, but more expensive to make. You need to rot the right type of item; show me an item, and I'll tell you if it's super- compostable or not.
textByHash.ae656597b0ced63456f873ed91659b56014f23e4: Tree seeds must be grown in a plantpot of soil into a sapling, and then transferred to a tree patch to continue growing to adulthood.
textByHash.a6edaedac0ebb731b3bb6159665e00c5cb4113ef: You can put up to ten potatoes, cabbages or onions in vegetable sacks, although you can't have a mix in the same sack.
textByHash.82b3bb051e232dbf89fa6df668611f47d4ad2bb4: You can fill plantpots with soil from any empty patch, if you have a gardening trowel.
textByHash.a29a52df2fc06db96d8448aaded4561eb6c73210: Bittercap mushrooms can only be grown in a special patch in Morytania, near the Mort Myre swamp. There the ground is especially dank and suited to growing poisonous fungii.
textByHash.89b3d09817bb8064531176f780ffdeb6fb5e6a03: Vegetables, hops and flowers need constant watering - if you ignore my advice, you will sooner or later find yourself in possession of a dead farming patch.
textByHash.cf3fc68dd3c639bc1ecba33aa905adf8d44e5bfa: You don't have to buy all your plantpots you know, you can make them yourself on a pottery wheel. If you're good enough at crafting, that is.
textByHash.5fa6c310e1dac42a67cc16e3c7f3fa5c7dd70f97: If you want to make your own sacks and baskets you'll need to use the loom that's near the Farming shop in Falador. If you're good enough at crafting, that is.
textByHash.a80ad5c92b7e28ba9c2f9ddbd8e001fd2701c38f: There are many Farming areas out there. I know of Elstan's area near Falador, Dantaera's area near Catherby, Kragen's area near Ardougne, Lyra's area in Morytania, Marisi's area in Hosidius and Alan's area in
textByHash.2fc762a3f48d3a93cc723827070ae8dae52916fb: the Farming Guild. That said, I'm sure there's plenty more that I don't know about.
textByHash.54045b3bbdd54aa75f5e896632323b9c1643a738: If you want to grow fruit trees you could try a few places: Catherby, Brimhaven, Gnome Stronghold and the Farming Guild all have fruit tree patches.
textByHash.55311f0bfe322285664c0c9f55e1c4011b33b9e4: You can put up to five tomatoes, strawberries, apples, bananas or oranges into a fruit basket, although you can't have a mix in the same basket.
textByHash.5f5d70ffca0e626c6fd81ee864018c689f4978a1: There is a special patch for growing Belladonna - I believe that it is somewhere near Draynor Manor, where the ground is a tad 'unblessed'.
textByHash.bdb606ebc97dab841a6849b38e89579be3605829: Applying compost to a patch will not only reduce the chance that your crops will get diseased, but you will also grow more crops to harvest.
textByHash.a948ca937c898af560bec30046158b9876f416f8: The only way to cure a bush or tree of disease is to prune away the diseased leaves with a pair of secateurs. For all other crops I would just apply some plant-cure.
textByHash.532c2f53eeb1eee0e9c290a18fd1128a373a915e: Hops are good for brewing ales. I believe there's a brewery up in Keldagrim somewhere, and I've heard rumours that a place called Phasmatys used to be good for that type of thing. 'Fore they all died, of course.
textByHash.0e23b2ce623239ece1296cca620e077ffe67cf10: Don't just throw away your weeds after you've raked a patch - put them in a compost bin and make some compost.
textByHash.1fc91822a6dd38a1807d1ec0d6aa54c61e2e9529: That depends on whether I have it to sell. What is it that you're looking for?
